question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getNode()] Can an unprivileged external user operating a malicious block node cause all BlockNode instances to be marked inactive via repeated errors, triggering the 'No block node can provide block' BlockStreamException that permanently halts block ingestion and freezes all fund movements?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onError()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly send malformed gRPC responses to exhaust the maxSubscribeAttempts counter on every configured BlockNode, causing all nodes to enter the inactive state simultaneously and permanently stalling block processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: streamBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node send a SubscribeStreamResponse with an unrecognized ResponseCase (default branch) to throw a BlockStreamException, increment the error counter, and if repeated across all nodes, permanently freeze fund processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: streamBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send a SubscribeStreamResponse.Code that is not SUCCESS (e.g., a non-zero error status) to throw BlockStreamException('Received status ... from block node'), and by repeating this across all nodes exhaust maxSubscribeAttempts and permanently halt block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: BlockAssembler.append()] Can an unprivileged external user flood a BlockItemSet with items exceeding streamProperties.getMaxBlockItems(), triggering BlockStreamException('Too many block items in a pending block'), and by repeating this on all nodes cause permanent cessation of block ingestion and fund freezing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: BlockAssembler.timeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that timeout() returns timeout.toMillis() - stopwatch.elapsed() (which can go negative) to cause grpcCall.read() to be called with a negative timeout, resulting in an immediate null return and silently skipping block assembly, permanently stalling the block number counter?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: hasBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user control a block node's serverStatus response to return a blockRange where lowerEndpoint() is a very large number, causing nextBlockNumber.set() to jump far ahead and skip all pending transactions, effectively freezing funds by making the importer believe blocks have already been processed?
